task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: An empty ContOnReset set means TPer_Reset_Stop is set for any reset condition and ReEncryptState is set to PAUSED. GeneralStatus value 34 means an active_reset_stop_detect condition, and value 5 means a pend_reset_stop_detect condition.

source_snippets.text: 5.7.2.2.17 ContOnReset This column value is a set of reset conditions. This value defines how a re-encryption process reacts to reset conditions. An empty set means the TPer_Reset_Stop condition is set for any reset condition. The ReEncryptState value is set to PAUSED. For each listed reset entry, the re-encryption process MAY continue after the associated reset is detected.

5.1.3.34 gen_status This set type is used to identify the general status of the re-encryption process. Table 86 gen_status | Column Value | Associated Value | Meaning | |--------------|------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 0 | None | | | 1 | pending_tper_error | Last ReEncryptState value was PENDING AND a TPer_Error_Detect condition was detected | | 2 | active_tper_error | Last ReEncryptState value was ACTIVE AND a TPer_Error_Detect condition was detected | | 3 | active_pause_requested | Last ReEncryptState value was ACTIVE AND PAUSE_req was detected | | 4 | pend_pause_requested | Last ReEncryptState value was PENDING AND a PAUSE_req value was detected | | 5 | pend_reset_stop_detect | A reset condition AND its associated ContOnReset configuration does not allow re-encryption to continue AND last state was PENDING | | 6 | key_error | ReEncryptState value was PENDING AND valid keys were not found in any C_* table OR insufficient access control granted for reading C_* table. | | 7 to 31 | reserved | | | 32 | wait_AvailableKeys | keys are not available | | 33 | wait_for_TPer_resources| TPer_Ready condition is not True | | 34 | active_reset_stop_detect | A reset condition AND its associated ContOnReset configuration does not allow re-encryption to continue AND last ReEncryptState value was ACTIVE | | 34 to 63 | reserved | | The enumeration values are associated as defined in table Table 87. Values 0-31 are valid for the PAUSED state, value 32-63 are valid for the PENDING state (see 5.7.3.3). Table 87 gen_status Enumeration Values ```markdown | Column Value | Associated Value | Meaning | |--------------|--------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 0 | None | | | 1 | pending_tper_error | Last ReEncryptState value was PENDING AND a TPer_Error_Detect condition was detected | | 2 | active_tper_error | Last ReEncryptState value was ACTIVE AND a TPer_Error_Detect condition was detected | | 3 | active_pause_requested | Last ReEncryptState value was ACTIVE AND PAUSE_req was detected | | 4 | pend_pause_requested | Last ReEncryptState value was PENDING AND a PAUSE_req value was detected | | 5 | pend_reset_stop_detect | A reset condition AND its associated ContOnReset configuration does not allow re-encryption to continue AND last state was PENDING | | 6 | key_error | ReEncryptState value was PENDING AND valid keys were not found in any C_* table OR insufficient access control granted for reading C_* table. | | 7 to 31 | reserved | | | 32 | wait_AvailableKeys | keys are not available | | 33 | wait_for_TPer_resources | TPer_Ready condition is not True | | 34 | active_reset_stop_detect | A reset condition AND its associated ContOnReset configuration does not allow re-encryption to continue AND last ReEncryptState value was ACTIVE | | 34 to 63 | reserved | | ```

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity
